title: Greedy Algorithms
analogy: A greedy algorithm is like always picking the biggest coin when making change — at each step you make the locally optimal choice, hoping it leads to a globally optimal solution.

overview.summary: Greedy algorithms build solutions piece by piece, always choosing the option that looks best at the current moment without reconsidering past choices.
learningObjectives: Distinguish problems solvable by greedy vs those requiring DP.
Prove greedy correctness via exchange argument.
overview.interviewRelevance: Greedy problems appear frequently in scheduling, interval, and optimization questions.

paragraphs: Huffman coding (used in ZIP compression) uses a greedy algorithm to assign shorter codes to more frequent characters.
Job scheduling in operating systems often uses greedy heuristics to maximize throughput.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: Greedy works when making the locally best choice at each step guarantees the globally best outcome.

definition.technicalDefinition: A greedy algorithm makes the locally optimal choice at each step with the hope of finding a global optimum. It never reconsiders previous decisions.

vocabulary.definition: A global optimum can be reached by making locally optimal choices.
A proof technique showing any non-greedy solution can be improved by swapping to the greedy choice.
A solution that satisfies all problem constraints.

keyProperties: Makes irrevocable decisions at each step.
Usually runs in O(N log N) due to sorting.
Does NOT work for all optimization problems — DP may be needed.

operations.name: Interval Scheduling
operations.description: Select the maximum number of non-overlapping intervals by always picking the one that ends earliest.
operations.visualNote: Intervals on a timeline, selected ones highlighted green, rejected ones crossed out.

practice.title: Jump Game
practice.keyInsight: Track the farthest reachable index greedily.

practice.title: Non-overlapping Intervals
practice.keyInsight: Sort by end time, count overlaps to remove.

practice.title: Assign Cookies
practice.keyInsight: Sort both arrays and match smallest cookie to smallest child.

practice.title: Gas Station
practice.keyInsight: If total gas >= total cost, a solution exists. Find the start greedily.

practice.title: Task Scheduler
practice.keyInsight: Greedy: schedule most frequent task first, fill cooling slots.

practice.title: Partition Labels
practice.keyInsight: Extend partition greedily until all chars' last occurrences are included.

conceptQuiz.question: When does greedy fail?
options: When locally optimal choices don't lead to a global optimum
conceptQuiz.explanation: Greedy fails when the problem lacks the greedy-choice property, e.g., 0/1 Knapsack.

recognitionQuiz.problemStatement: Given a set of meetings with start and end times, find the maximum number of non-overlapping meetings you can attend.
recognitionQuiz.explanation: Interval scheduling with earliest-end-time selection is the textbook greedy problem.
